name: Monte Hijedo Trail (PRC-BU 30)
name_original: Monte Hijedo – ruta circular hasta la Cabaña de Hijedo
description: Hike the moderate 11.0-mile Monte Hijedo Trail (PRC-BU 30) through one of Spain's most important oak forests, gaining 1040 feet.
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails around Monte Hijedo traverse one of northern Spain's most extensive and well-preserved deciduous forests, characterized by ancient sessile oaks, beech trees, and yews. The landscape features diverse terrain, including unique sandstone formations near Santa Gadea and open peat bogs. The Hijedo River flows through the area, contributing to its ecological richness, while the nearby Ebro Reservoir offers expansive views. Best family friendly hiking trails around Monte Hijedo The most popular family friendly hiking route is Monte Hijedo Trail (PRC-BU 30), a 10.9 miles (17.6 km) trail that takes about 4 hours to complete. This route leads through significant oak forests and offers moderate elevation gains. Another top favourite among local hikers is Monte de Hijedo – Hijedo Cabin loop from Alfoz de Santa Gadea, a moderate 6.9 miles (11.2 km) path. This trail explores varied forest landscapes and passes by the historic Cabaña de Hijedo complex. Local hikers also love the Embalse del Ebro and Camino Olvidado Loop, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail leading through reservoir surroundings and historical sites, often completed in about 4 hours. Hiking around Monte Hijedo is defined by ancient deciduous forests, unique sandstone formations, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate forest paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Monte Hijedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 90 hikers have used komoot to explore Monte Hijedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available around Monte Hijedo?
A: There are 6 family-friendly hiking trails detailed in this guide for the Monte Hijedo area. These routes offer a variety of experiences suitable for families looking to explore the region's natural beauty.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking with family in Monte Hijedo?
A: Monte Hijedo is particularly captivating in &lt;strong&gt;spring&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;autumn&lt;/strong&gt;. Spring offers a fresh atmosphere with new growth, while autumn showcases stunning changing colors in the extensive deciduous forests, making both seasons ideal for family hikes.

Q: Are there any easy routes suitable for families with younger children or beginners?
A: While most routes in this guide are moderate, the Monte Hijedo area offers paths suitable for various fitness levels. For a slightly shorter, moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de Hijedo – Hijedo Cabin loop from Alfoz de Santa Gadea&lt;/a&gt;, which is around 11 km long and takes about 3 hours and 20 minutes.

Q: What kind of natural features can we expect to see on family hikes in Monte Hijedo?
A: Family hikes in Monte Hijedo will immerse you in one of northern Spain's most extensive and best-preserved deciduous forests, featuring ancient sessile oaks, impressive beech trees, and even centuries-old yews. You might also encounter unique sandstone formations near Santa Gadea, the serene Hijedo River, and expansive views of the Ebro Reservoir on some routes.

Q: Are there any circular routes suitable for families?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, which is often convenient for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de Hijedo – Hijedo Cabin loop from Alfoz de Santa Gadea&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering a loop through the forest. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046458215/embalse-del-ebro-and-camino-olvidado-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Ebro and Camino Olvidado Loop&lt;/a&gt;, providing reservoir views and historical sights on a circular path.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the family-friendly trails in Monte Hijedo?
A: The Monte Hijedo area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning natural beauty of the ancient forests, the well-marked trails, and the peaceful atmosphere, making it a wonderful destination for family outings.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest along the family trails?
A: Absolutely! A significant landmark within the forest is the &lt;strong&gt;Cabaña de Hijedo&lt;/strong&gt;, a complex featuring a large house, stables, a chapel, and an ancient, centuries-old yew tree. Many trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996897215/monte-hijedo-trail-prc-bu-30" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Hijedo Trail (PRC-BU 30)&lt;/a&gt;, pass by or near this historic site. There are also dedicated viewpoints offering panoramic vistas of the expansive forest.

Q: What are the typical durations and elevation gains for family hikes in this area?
A: The family-friendly hikes in Monte Hijedo vary. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046458215/embalse-del-ebro-and-camino-olvidado-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Ebro and Camino Olvidado Loop&lt;/a&gt; is about 14.8 km with around 190 meters of elevation gain, taking approximately 4 hours. Other moderate routes can be up to 17 km with around 300 meters of elevation, typically taking 3.5 to 4.5 hours. There is also one difficult route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046450659/monte-hijedo-forest-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Hijedo Forest Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 17.4 km with 437 meters of elevation gain, taking about 5 hours.

Q: Is there a visitor center or information point for Monte Hijedo?
A: Yes, a visitor center for Monte Hijedo is located in Riopanero. It serves as a helpful entry point to the area and can provide additional information for your family's hiking plans.

Q: What should we pack for a family hike in Monte Hijedo?
A: Given the varied terrain and forest environment, it's advisable to pack appropriate footwear, layers of clothing suitable for changing weather, water, snacks, and a first-aid kit. Depending on the season, insect repellent might also be useful. Always check the weather forecast before heading out.
tips: Located in the heart of the beautiful Mount Hijedo, the Cabin is a small palace with typical regional architecture that was built at the beginning of the 20th century and next to the main building is the small hermitage.
Monte de Hijedo is a true natural paradise. It is a very quiet and picturesque place that can be explored following a marked path that offers an ideal itinerary for the whole family.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Monte Hijedo Trail (PRC-BU 30)?
faqs.answer: The Monte Hijedo Trail (PRC-BU 30) is a moderate hike that generally takes around 4 hours to complete. While some sources suggest a shorter duration of 2.5 to 3 hours for a slightly shorter route, the full 17.6 km loop as mapped typically requires about 4 hours.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail winds through a diverse mixed forest, primarily featuring pedunculate oaks, but also beeches, alders, birches, hazelnut trees, and impressive centuries-old yews. You'll encounter sections of comfortable wide forest tracks, narrower forest paths, and parts that run alongside the Hijedo stream. The landscape also includes curious rocky formations like shelters and 'rock mushrooms' sculpted by erosion. Be aware that some sections might become muddy, especially after rain.

faqs.question: Is the Monte Hijedo Trail suitable for families or beginner hikers?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered easy to moderate and is suitable for families. The elevation gain is manageable, and the path is well-marked with red and blue arrows, as well as yellow and white signs, making navigation straightforward. While it's a longer route, the overall difficulty is not high.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to hike the Monte Hijedo Trail?
faqs.answer: Autumn is particularly recommended for hiking the Monte Hijedo Trail due to the vibrant colors of the deciduous trees, creating a stunning landscape. Spring also offers beautiful scenery with fresh greenery. While accessible year-round, be mindful of potential mud during wetter seasons.

faqs.question: What notable features or viewpoints can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several points of interest. You'll pass through the ancient Monte de Hijedo, one of Spain's most important oak forests. A significant historical feature is the Hijedo Cabin, a unique building that once served multiple purposes. There's also a notable viewpoint offering expansive views over a large portion of the Monte Hijedo, bridging the provinces of Burgos and Cantabria.

faqs.question: Is the Monte Hijedo Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Monte Hijedo Trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers, especially given the presence of animals like roe deer, wild boars, and even wolves in the area.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to access the Monte Hijedo Trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Monte Hijedo Trail (PRC-BU 30). It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided in the available information, trails of this nature typically have designated parking areas near the trailhead in the vicinity of Monte Hijedo. Look for signs indicating parking for the PRC-BU 30 route.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The Monte Hijedo forest is a Natura 2000 site, known for its rich biodiversity. Hikers might spot a variety of wildlife, including roe deer, wild boars, otters, and even wolves. Birdwatching opportunities are also excellent due to the diverse forest ecosystem.

faqs.question: Is the Monte Hijedo Trail a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Monte Hijedo Trail (PRC-BU 30) is a circular route, meaning it loops back to its starting point. This allows for a continuous hiking experience without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Monte Hijedo trail.
